summary: Draft the per-layer retrieval isolation bake-off spec (candidates + metric + GATE-0 canary) for I-ret-002, §-1.1-compliance critiqued

specs.holding_condition: IterResearch (Tongyi) queries are HELD FIXED — the I-qgen-001 winner wired as PG_QGEN_ITERRESEARCH (commit 84bb2d86) — and the SAME IterResearch query set feeds every candidate via the deterministic per-query snapshot cache (outputs/qgen_coverage/retrieval_cache pattern, keyed by sha256(query)). Everything downstream of search stays at current POLARIS default: fetch (crawl4ai + Zyte), extract (Trafilatura + clean_fetch_body line strip), near-dup dedup, the lexical rerank, tier/authority weight, finding_dedup/fact_dedup consolidation/baskets, AND the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance). ONLY the search backend varies: the `_serper_search` call in live_retriever.py (SERPER_ENDPOINT) is abstracted behind a `SearchProvider.search(query) -> ranked[url, title, snippet]` adapter (src/search/serper_client.py is the existing seam); each candidate is one adapter. CRITICAL ISOLATION POINT: the score is taken on the RANKED URL RESULT LIST that search returns, BEFORE fetch/extract — so this measures discovery+ranking, not the fetch lane (a separate section's bake-off). Corpus-access APIs (OpenAlex / PubMed / arXiv) are held FIXED as the academic access lane, NOT bake-off candidates, because they are unranked access paths; the one exception is Semantic Scholar's relevance-ranked /search endpoint, which IS a ranked search and so enters as a candidate (justified ranking comparison vs Serper web rank). Budget identical per candidate: same query rounds, same per-query result k (POLARIS default), same wall/token caps.

candidates.name: Serper (Google SERP) — current POLARIS search backend
candidates.license: Proprietary SaaS (operator-keyed); query API, not a model
candidates.impl_notes: THE FLOOR every candidate must beat. Already the live-path provider. Adapter just returns the existing ranked items (url/title/snippet) without fetch. Hold num=PG default; paginate to PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY exactly as production. No GPU.

candidates.name: Exa (neural/long-tail search)
candidates.impl_id: pip: exa-py ; POST https://api.exa.ai/search (EXA_API_KEY held); endpoint type='neural'/'auto'
candidates.impl_notes: Neural embedding-ranked search — the long-tail recall lever the landscape doc names as a parallel-to-Serper add. Adapter calls /search with the FIXED IterResearch query, returns results[].url ranked, NO contents fetch (use_autoprompt=False to keep the held-fixed query verbatim — do NOT let Exa rewrite the query, that would break the holding condition). No GPU.

candidates.name: Semantic Scholar relevance-ranked search
candidates.impl_notes: Justified ranking candidate (NOT a raw access path): /paper/search returns relevance-RANKED papers, distinct from unranked OpenAlex/PubMed access. Adapter returns ranked externalIds.DOI->URL + openAccessPdf.url. Academic-only recall; expected strong on the DRB-II named-study findings, weak on gov/news findings — that contrast is the signal. No GPU.

candidates.name: Firecrawl search
candidates.license: Proprietary SaaS (operator-keyed); code MIT, hosted search is SaaS
candidates.impl_notes: Agent-search API returning ranked web results. Adapter calls /v2/search with scrapeOptions DISABLED (search-only, no fetch) so we score the URL ranking before the held-fixed POLARIS fetch lane. No GPU.

candidates.name: SearXNG (sovereign metasearch, self-host)
candidates.impl_id: Docker: searxng/searxng (docker pull searxng/searxng); local JSON API GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/search?format=json
candidates.impl_notes: The ONLY sovereign search option (landscape §6). Self-host on the VM as a container; aggregates Google/Bing/arXiv/PubMed/S2 engines. runnable=needs_gpu marks 'must stand up the VM service' (it is CPU-only, no GPU model — flag is honest 'needs the VM container up', not a model GPU need). Adapter hits the local JSON API with the FIXED query; configure a stable engine set + pin the container image sha so the snapshot is reproducible. ~0.5GB RAM, 0 GPU.

candidates.impl_notes: De-facto 2025/26 agent-search API (LLM-ready excerpts). NOT tested — no key. Noted honestly, not faked. Add to the bake-off only if a key is provisioned.

candidates.impl_notes: Agent-search API returning ranked LLM-ready results. NOT tested — no key. Noted, not faked.

candidates.name: Brave Search
candidates.impl_notes: The one large INDEPENDENT Western index after Bing Search retired 2025-08-11 (landscape §10) — strong recall yardstick. NOT tested — no key. Noted honestly. Non-sovereign, so a yardstick not an adoption candidate.

metric.name: Gold-source recall@k (per required-finding, on the ranked URL list pre-fetch)
metric.definition: For each DRB-II info_recall required finding f (idx 56/62/66/72), the gold fixture names f's canonical source URL/DOI (gold_url(f)). For a candidate search backend, run the FIXED IterResearch query set, collect the union of ranked URL results within rank-k (k = the held-identical POLARIS per-query result budget). Score(f) = 1 iff normalized(gold_url(f)) appears in that ranked result set, else 0; recall = mean over all f. URL normalization (reuse harness _normalize_url + DOI-canonicalization) so a publisher mirror / ?utm / trailing-slash variant of the gold source still matches. The DRB-II blocked source is excluded before scoring (reuse make_blocked_filter) so no candidate scores via the forbidden source. Paired per-finding-per-task; bootstrap CI + Holm/FDR vs the Serper baseline + a locked minimum-effect threshold; finalists rerun >=3x on the frozen per-query snapshot. The result list is the REAL output of each search backend.
metric.ground_truth_source: DRB-II tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl info_recall rubrics (third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II) — the SAME ground truth the query-gen bake-off used — resolved to per-finding canonical source URLs in a NEW labeled fixture (drb_gold_sources.jsonl). The info_recall items are prose findings that NAME specific studies by title (verified: idx56 has 57 findings naming ~14 distinct studies, ZERO embedded URLs), so the title->URL/DOI resolution is the fixture to build.
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NOT a banned count/pattern/metadata proxy: it never rewards 'how many sources' or 'does term X appear' — a backend returning 500 junk URLs and 0 gold sources scores 0.0, a backend returning 5 URLs that include every gold source scores 1.0. It is per-finding recall against a per-finding LABELED gold source (the DRB-II info_recall ground truth), scored at the discovery seam (URL surfaced) instead of the corpus-text seam, with EVERY required finding scored (no sampling) and full rubric text retained for the audit. It is not a metadata comparison between candidates; each is scored independently against the fixed gold.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: This layer changes ONLY which backend lists URLs before fetch; strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance are downstream and never invoked in this isolation test, so they cannot be touched or relaxed. No candidate can lift recall by weakening a faithfulness gate (the gates don't run here), and search rank enters POLARIS as a WEIGHT into consolidation — never a hard drop (§-1.3); the only hard drop is the unchanged obvious-junk structural floor. The winning backend is later re-validated e2e where the faithfulness gates do run, before adoption.

specs.gate0_canary: Four fail-loud checks; the harness is GREEN (and no candidate score is trusted) only when ALL pass. (1) POSITIVE — feed a synthetic ranked URL list KNOWN to contain every gold source URL for the idx; recall MUST equal 1.000 exactly. (2) NEGATIVE — feed a junk-only ranked list with NO gold URL; recall MUST equal 0.000 exactly (proves the scorer grants zero fake credit — the drb_72 'fake numbers that look real' guard). (3) URL-NORMALIZATION canary — present the gold DOI as a publisher mirror / trailing-slash / ?utm= variant; it MUST still match recall=1.0 (proves the normalizer is not silently MISSING real hits, the inverse disaster that would understate every candidate). (4) LINEAGE canary — reuse gate0_lineage: assert launched==packed==answered==canonical idx so recall is scored against the RIGHT task's findings and the RIGHT gold fixture row; a wrong-idx or wrong-fixture binding fails loud (the drb_72 wrong-question structural guard).
specs.ground_truth_to_build: BUILD FIRST — drb_gold_sources.jsonl (the one prerequisite; it does not exist). For each registered DRB-II idx (56/62/66/72) map each info_recall finding -> its canonical gold source URL/DOI. Size: ~14 distinct named studies per task -> ~50-60 gold sources total. Resolve each quoted study title via Crossref + OpenAlex title lookup, VERIFY the DOI resolves and the returned title matches the rubric title (fail loud on ambiguity, never guess), exclude the DRB-II blocked source. Freeze + sha-pin the fixture in the lineage manifest so the gold cannot drift. The rest is reuse: the per-query deterministic snapshot cache, gate0_lineage canonical binding, make_blocked_filter, and _normalize_url already exist in scripts/dr_benchmark/.
specs.notes: Reuses the I-qgen-001 coverage harness machinery but INVERTS the variable: query-gen held retrieve() fixed and varied queries; search_discovery holds IterResearch queries fixed and varies the search backend at the _serper_search seam. The score is taken on the RANKED URL LIST PRE-FETCH (not corpus text) precisely to isolate discovery/ranking from the held-fixed fetch+extract lane — this is the cleaner isolation point and matches the task's stated metric direction (recall of required-finding SOURCE URLs). The task's metric direction is honored, but its phrasing ('recall of the DRB-II required-finding SOURCE URLs') assumes the gold file already lists URLs; it does NOT (info_recall items are prose findings naming studies by title, verified zero URLs), which is exactly why drb_gold_sources.jsonl must be built and is flagged needs_fixture. Sovereignty read: SearXNG is the only sovereign candidate; Serper/Exa/S2/Firecrawl/Tavily/Parallel/Brave are non-sovereign — a strong non-sovereign winner is a yardstick that informs which engines to configure inside SearXNG, not necessarily the adopted backend. Run candidates in parallel (cost not the constraint); single-writer per snapshot; orphan-process audit between runs (§8.4).

specs.layer: fetch_crawl (fetch + unblock engine; the stage that turns a discovered URL into a retrieved page body, before extraction/strip/weight)
specs.holding_condition: FIXED for every candidate: (1) IterResearch/Tongyi query set (the I-qgen-001 winner, flag PG_QGEN_ITERRESEARCH ON) — identical queries; (2) Serper search at POLARIS default — so the SAME ranked URL set (the search-winner output, frozen to a banked url_list.json per topic) is the input to every fetch engine; (3) extraction held CONSTANT at Trafilatura 2.0.0 (safe_trafilatura_extract, the content-of-record) applied identically to whatever HTML/markdown each engine returns, so the bake-off measures FETCH (did the real body come back vs a 403/login-wall/stub), not extractor differences; (4) clean_fetch_body line-level strip, ContentDeduplicator, tier/authority weighting, consolidation, and the faithfulness engine all at current POLARIS default and BYPASSED for scoring (we score the raw recovered body against the reference, upstream of weight). ONLY the fetch/unblock engine varies. Each engine is driven in SINGLE-ENGINE mode (no fallback cascade) so a candidate's score is its own, not the integrated cascade's.

candidates.name: crawl4ai (sovereign default)
candidates.impl_id: pip install crawl4ai==0.9.0 ; then crawl4ai-setup (installs Playwright chromium)
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: 0 GB GPU (CPU + headless chromium, ~1-2 GB RAM per browser; cap PG_CRAWL4AI_CONCURRENCY=2 per §8.4). 'needs_gpu' only in the sense it runs on the GPU VM for parity, no model loaded.
candidates.impl_notes: from crawl4ai import AsyncWebCrawler; async with AsyncWebCrawler() as c: res = await c.arun(url); feed res.html into safe_trafilatura_extract for the held-constant extraction. This IS the current POLARIS sovereign fetch engine (access_bypass.py crawl4ai path) — it is the FLOOR every candidate must beat. Bound concurrency to 2 browsers; reuse reset_crawl4ai_semaphore_state between topics.

candidates.impl_id: pip install zyte-api==0.10.0 ; key from ZYTE_API_KEY in /home/ubuntu/polaris_run/.env on the VM
candidates.license: proprietary SaaS (operator has a paid key; per landscape §5 the one place no OSS equal exists)
candidates.impl_notes: from zyte_api import AsyncZyteAPI; request {'url': url, 'browserHtml': true} (render) — for paywalled journals also try {'httpResponseBody': true}. Feed browserHtml into the SAME safe_trafilatura_extract. MUST copy ZYTE_API_KEY + PG_ZYTE* from polaris_run/.env into the bake-off env or it silently no-ops (per fact_zyte_key_location memory). Marked no_key because the key lives only on the VM, not this workstation. This is the lever expected to win on the paywalled/anti-bot URL stratum.

candidates.impl_id: pip install firecrawl-py==4.30.2 ; key from FIRECRAWL_API_KEY
candidates.license: proprietary SaaS (open-source self-host core is AGPL-3.0; the hosted API used here is proprietary)

Note the access_bypass firecrawl circuit-breaker already exists. no_key: needs FIRECRAWL_API_KEY in the bake-off env.

candidates.name: Playwright (raw, no anti-bot service)
candidates.impl_id: pip install playwright==1.60.0 ; python -m playwright install chromium
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: 0 GB GPU (~1-2 GB RAM per chromium context; run headless, cap concurrency)
candidates.impl_notes: from playwright.async_api import async_playwright; page.goto(url, wait_until='networkidle'); html = await page.content(); feed into safe_trafilatura_extract. The RAW-render floor: JS rendering with no paywall/anti-bot unblock and no boilerplate handling — isolates 'how much does the Zyte/Firecrawl UNBLOCK layer add over plain headless render'. Lower bound for the WALLED stratum.

metric.name: Per-URL body-recovery verdict + main-content reference-recall (judged on a FIXED URL set against a labeled reference body)
metric.definition: On the FROZEN url_list.json (identical for every engine), two judged quantities per URL, both against the labeled fixture — never against a count or a length floor: (A) RECOVERY VERDICT in {RECOVERED, WALLED (403/login/paywall-stub), SOFT_STUB (200 but boilerplate/error-shell/empty-after-clean), FETCH_ERROR}, assigned by comparing the engine's returned body to the fixture's labeled outcome class + the human-labeled reference main-content; the fixture's gold class is the ground truth, the engine's body is matched to it. (B) COMPLETENESS = main-content reference-recall = fraction of the labeled reference main-content (sentence/long-span level) that is present, verbatim, in the engine's returned body (WebMainBench-style longest-common-span / ROUGE-recall over the GOLD body span, in [0,1]); this is recall of the KNOWN-correct article body, not a char count and not a threshold. Layer score per engine = mean COMPLETENESS over the fixed set (a URL the engine WALLED or STUBbed scores its true low recall, so failures are penalised by the same continuous reference-recall, no separate count). Stratified report: recovery-verdict distribution + mean completeness within each URL stratum (OA-journal / paywalled-journal / gov-org / news / social-SEO) so a Zyte win on the paywalled stratum is visible and not washed out. WINNER = highest mean reference-recall with NO recall regression (no real article body the floor recovered that the candidate loses) on the OA/gov strata — a candidate that gains paywalled bodies but drops OA bodies is rejected (recall-preservation gate).
metric.ground_truth_source: A net-new labeled fixture (fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture) — see ground_truth_to_build. URLs are drawn from the banked corpus snapshots (outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/drb_72|drb_76|drb_90/corpus_snapshot.json: each has retrieval.classified_sources = 849/… real URLs with tier_reasons like 'Fetched body is 377 chars (<1000) -> T7 stub'), but the corpus snapshots store URLs + tier-reasons ONLY, NOT reference bodies — so the human-labeled reference main-content per URL is the net-new ground truth. Reference labeling method = WebMainBench/MainWebBench protocol (human-marked main-content span per page), the independent extraction yardstick named in retrieval_landscape_2026.md §10.
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NOT a banned count/pattern/metadata proxy: (1) the denominator is a FIXED, identical URL set, so the score can never be gamed by 'fetch more URLs' — every engine is judged on the exact same pages, so this is per-URL judged QUALITY against ground truth, not 'how many sources'; (2) COMPLETENESS is reference-RECALL of a human-labeled correct body span, the §-1.1-sanctioned 'real corpus quality against ground truth (junk-reduction / recall-preservation)' construction — it is NOT a length threshold (a 1200-char floor would be the banned string/threshold proxy), NOT pattern-presence ('does the page mention X'), NOT a metadata comparison ('engine A has N more sources'); (3) it is FULL-set, not sample-based — every URL in the fixture is judged, no 'we audited 5 of 50'; (4) the recovery verdict is a per-URL adjudication against gold (RECOVERED/WALLED/STUB/ERROR with the labeled reference), the §-1.1 per-item verdict shape, not a string-presence PASS/FAIL gate-table.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: Fetch is byte-deterministic: every candidate returns RAW page bytes, then the SAME held-constant Trafilatura extractor runs — no LLM rewrite of any span at any candidate, so a verbatim span the strict_verify/NLI/provenance gate later checks is never altered by this layer. Firecrawl's and Zyte's own LLM/auto-extract outputs are explicitly DISCARDED (we consume their fetch HTML only) precisely to avoid the fabrication/omission hazard the landscape §5 'DO NOT add LLM-semantic extractors as content-of-record' flags. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / span-grounding / NLI / 4-role / provenance) is BYPASSED for scoring and entirely UNTOUCHED — this layer only changes WHICH true bytes arrive, never how they are verified; recovering MORE real body can only help the downstream gate, never relax it.

specs.gate0_canary: Two-extreme pre-banked separation canary that proves the harness scores a KNOWN input correctly BEFORE any engine is trusted (the drb_72 'fake numbers that look real' guard). Feed the scorer two banked bodies whose correct scores are already known, bypassing all four fetch engines: (1) KNOWN-GOOD = the real AEA jep.33.2.3 automation/labor article body (the REAL fixture already in outputs/audits/drb_72_2026_06_12/runA_final/evidence_pool.json and the idx49 harness), with gold verdict=RECOVERED and gold reference=its own body → MUST score completeness ~1.0; AND a lightly-truncated copy of that same body (first 60% only) → MUST score completeness ~0.6, proving the recall metric is continuous and not a 0/1 threshold. (2) KNOWN-JUNK = the banked scirp.org reference-listing shell body from the same pool (clean_fetch_body returns shell_reason='empty_after_clean'; is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional flags it), with gold verdict=SOFT_STUB → MUST score completeness ~0.0 and verdict=SOFT_STUB. The harness PASSES (and only then are candidates run) iff it scores good≈1.0, truncated≈0.6, junk≈0.0 AND the per-URL recovery verdicts match gold. FAIL-LOUD non-zero exit if the scorer cannot separate these three pre-known extremes — a harness that can't tell the AEA article from the scirp shell, or can't see the 60% truncation, is the wrong-question/Title-only-stub/split-brain bug and no candidate score is believed. Reuse the existing deterministic detectors (clean_fetch_body.shell_reason, is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional in access_bypass.py:1437) as the independent cross-check that the junk gold label is itself correct.
specs.ground_truth_to_build: BUILD IT — fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture (net-new; the corpus snapshots have URLs but NOT reference bodies). Size: ~60-100 URLs, sampled stratified across 5 strata (OA-journal, paywalled-journal, gov/.org, news, social/SEO-spam) by drawing real URLs from the banked retrieval.classified_sources lists in outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/drb_72_ai_labor + drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc + drb_90_adas_liability/corpus_snapshot.json (849 + … real URLs already classified, incl. confirmed stubs like the sciencedirect '377-char T7 stub' and the scirp shell). Per URL, label: (a) the human-verified main-content reference span (WebMainBench/MainWebBench marking protocol — the independent yardstick from landscape §10), and (b) the gold recovery verdict {RECOVERED/WALLED/SOFT_STUB/FETCH_ERROR} fixed at fixture-build time from a one-time render. Seed the labeling with the AEA-real + scirp-shell bodies already banked in the drb_72 evidence_pool.json / idx49 harness (those become the GATE-0 canary rows, already verified). Honest note: because live pages drift (a paywall/wall state can change), re-snapshot raw HTML per URL into the fixture at build time and score engines against that frozen snapshot for the wall/stub strata, while the RECOVERY verdict is re-judged live to measure each engine's real unblock power — both numbers reported. Store under tests/fixtures/fetch_crawl_refbody/ (real-data fixture, allowed there per LAW VI).
specs.notes: Isolation honored: no report generation, no render, no DeepTRACE judge — pure fetch→(held Trafilatura)→score-vs-reference. Candidate runnability is honest: crawl4ai + Playwright are Apache-2.0 and run on the GPU VM with chromium (no model, ~1-2 GB RAM/browser, concurrency-capped per §8.4); Zyte + Firecrawl are no_key from THIS workstation (keys live in /home/ubuntu/polaris_run/.env on the VM) and are remote APIs (0 GB local). crawl4ai is the BASELINE FLOOR (current POLARIS sovereign engine) and Playwright-raw is the second floor (render-without-unblock), so the bake-off always includes the current behaviour, not just new candidates (per the standard-process 'verified baselines AND the current POLARIS behaviour as the floor' rule). Weight-not-filter (§-1.3) is respected: this layer applies NO quality drop — the only hard drop in the whole stack is the obvious-junk structural floor (clean_fetch_body shell + junk_detection), which is held at default and not part of the bake-off; a SOFT_STUB scores low reference-recall (a measurement) but is never hard-dropped by the metric. Sovereignty caveat surfaced honestly: Zyte/Firecrawl are non-sovereign — if Zyte wins only the paywalled stratum, the decision is 'keep crawl4ai sovereign default + Zyte as the keyed unblock fallback for paywalled URLs' (the current architecture), not 'replace the sovereign engine'. The standalone number SELECTS; the integrated combined run (all section winners ON) DECIDES, per the standard process §44-49. GitHub anchor: I-ret-002 #1294, retrieval section of docs/standard_process_pipeline_section_review.md. Relevant code: src/tools/access_bypass.py (fetch_with_bypass:2134 cascade — driven SINGLE-ENGINE here; clean_fetch_body:1661; is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional:1437; safe_trafilatura_extract:943); existing behavioral harness pattern to mirror: scripts/iarch_beatboth011_idx49_fetchshell_evidence_harness.py.

specs.holding_condition: ISOLATION on the extraction layer only. HELD FIXED: (1) IterResearch/Tongyi queries (PG_QGEN_ITERRESEARCH, the I-qgen-001 winner) and the entire rest of the retrieval stack — Serper discovery, crawl4ai+Zyte fetch/unblock — at current POLARIS default, so every candidate receives BYTE-IDENTICAL fetched HTML for each page (the same banked raw-HTML snapshot is the shared input). (2) The line-level strip clean_fetch_body/strip_web_boilerplate (access_bypass.py:1661/1412) is held fixed and applied IDENTICALLY downstream of every extractor (same code, same patterns) — we vary ONLY the content-of-record extractor that produces the body the strip then cleans; junk-reduction differences therefore attribute to the extractor, not the strip. (3) Nothing downstream is re-run: there is NO basket/consolidation, NO generator (multi_section), NO verify, NO render — the metric scores extractor OUTPUT directly against gold. (4) The faithfulness engine (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance) is not invoked or modified. Only the extractor binary varies across candidates; all else is pinned by sha256 lineage manifest (extractor id+version, HTML snapshot id, strip code rev, scorer config, gold rev).

candidates.name: Trafilatura 2.x (incumbent content-of-record)
candidates.impl_id: pip: trafilatura==2.1.0 (Apache-2.0); called via POLARIS safe_trafilatura_extract (access_bypass.py:943)
candidates.impl_notes: THE current POLARIS extractor of record and the floor every candidate must beat. Run trafilatura.extract(html, output_format='markdown', include_tables=True, include_links=False) through the existing guarded entrypoint so the bench matches production behavior exactly (size-gate + SIGSEGV containment via PG_TRAFILATURA_SUBPROCESS=1). Published WebMainBench Html+MD F1=0.6402 (Dripper paper Table 2, arXiv 2511.23119) — this is the recall/precision NON-REGRESSION floor (advisor pt E).

candidates.name: MinerU-HTML (Dripper, SLM structured/clinical lane)
candidates.impl_id: HF model: opendatalab/MinerU-HTML (v1.1, base Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B); pip: mineru-html (pip install .[baselines] from github.com/opendatalab/MinerU-HTML); paper arXiv 2511.23119
candidates.license: Apache-2.0 (code AND model weights — verified on HF README, OpenDatalab Shanghai AILab 2025)
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: ~4 GB (Qwen3-0.6B fp16 via vLLM; allocate ~8GB headroom)
candidates.impl_notes: LEAD candidate for the clinical/table lane. CONFIRMED EXTRACTIVE not generative (Dripper paper: classifies DOM blocks main/other via constrained JSON decoding {"1":"main",...} then reconstructs C={mi in M | y_i=1} from the ORIGINAL DOM — text is verbatim, no paraphrase, 'eliminates format hallucinations') → faithfulness-safe as content-of-record. Serve via vLLM (VLLM_USE_V1=1 for the state machine). Published WebMainBench F1=0.8779 (fallback variant 0.8925), TABLE fidelity TEDS 0.739 vs Trafilatura 0.341 — the load-bearing edge for clinical dose/trial tables that ROUGE alone misses (see TEDS sub-metric).

candidates.name: Resiliparse (recall-biased ensemble partner)
candidates.impl_notes: Fast C++ heuristic extractor; the recall-biased partner in the union (per 'Beyond a Single Extractor' arXiv 2602.19548 — only ~39% of pages survive all extractors, so union raises token yield). Published WebMainBench TEXT F1=0.6290 (Dripper Table 2) — also the SECONDARY GATE-0 anchor.

candidates.name: jusText (3rd union member)
candidates.impl_notes: Heuristic boilerplate-removal extractor; the third member of the Apple union. MUST set language='English' (stoplist) for the clinical fixture — its published 0.4783 is partly a multilingual mis-set artifact; do not tank it unfairly (advisor pt F). En-set so junk-removal is measured on-domain.

candidates.name: readability-lxml (last-resort fallback baseline)
candidates.impl_notes: The 2010 DOM-scoring heuristic POLARIS keeps ONLY as last-resort fallback after Trafilatura. Benched as the second floor (what production falls back to). Published WebMainBench Html+MD F1=0.6543 (Dripper Table 2) — a GATE-0 anchor. Flagged dated in the recency audit; included so the bake-off proves the upgrade beats BOTH current floors, not just the primary.

candidates.name: union(trafilatura+resiliparse+justext) — deterministic ensemble
candidates.impl_id: composite (no single package): deterministic merge of the three extractor outputs
candidates.impl_notes: NOT a package — define deterministic semantics: run all three extractors on the same HTML, take the line-level UNION of their main-body lines, then near-dup collapse (datasketch MinHash, fixed seed/threshold) to drop lines >=3 extractors duplicate. Pure-extractive (all members verbatim) → faithfulness-safe. Tests the 2602.19548 'one extractor never suffices' deployment pattern for recall-max; the precision cost is exactly what the precision/recall split measures.

candidates.name: ReaderLM-v2 (generative upper-bound yardstick — NEVER content-of-record)
candidates.impl_notes: GENERATIVE (rewrites text token-by-token) → a fabrication/omission hazard for a provenance-gated pipeline; benched ONLY as the non-sovereign upper-bound that concretely demonstrates the never-crown guard (faithfulness rule). Even if its ROUGE-F1 tops the board it is STRUCTURALLY DISQUALIFIED as content-of-record because strict_verify needs verbatim spans. Its purpose is to prove the decision rule refuses a higher number when verbatim-preservation fails — the §-1.1 anti-gaming demonstration.

metric.name: Gold-referenced extraction precision/recall + ROUGE-N F1 (general) + TEDS table-fidelity (clinical subset)
metric.definition: For each fetched page with a human-labeled gold main body: PRIMARY = WebMainBench's OFFICIAL ROUGE-N F1 scorer (N=5, jieba-tokenized — the paper's exact config, NOT hand-rolled) between candidate extractor output and gold Markdown. To make junk-reduction and completeness §-1.1-legal we DECOMPOSE F1 into its two reportable halves: COMPLETENESS/main-body recall = ROUGE-N recall of candidate vs gold (did real main content survive); JUNK = 1 - precision = fraction of candidate n-grams NOT present in the gold main body (chrome/nav/ads/SEO boilerplate that leaked in). 'Junk reduction' = the candidate's (1-precision) is LOWER than the Trafilatura-incumbent baseline's (1-precision) ON THE SAME pages. SECONDARY (clinical table subset only) = TEDS (Tree-Edit-Distance Similarity) of the extracted table tree vs the gold table tree — captures row/col structure that n-gram ROUGE cannot (a dose table flattened to the right words in the wrong cells scores high on ROUGE but is clinically dangerous). DECISION RULE: among candidates that do NOT regress recall below the Trafilatura incumbent floor (minus tolerance) — the weight-not-filter recall floor (§-1.3) — winner = highest F1, with ties broken toward the DETERMINISTIC/extractive extractor (never the generative one). A precision gain bought by recall loss does NOT auto-win. Paired bootstrap CIs + Holm/FDR + minimum-effect threshold over the locked page list decide significance (mirrors I-qgen-001).
metric.ground_truth_source: PRIMARY general yardstick + GATE-0 anchor: WebMainBench (OpenDataLab, 7,809 HTML-tag-level human-annotated pages with gold Markdown, ROUGE-N F1; the same set whose per-extractor numbers are published in Dripper arXiv 2511.23119 Table 2). Filter to its biomedical/scientific domains where extractable. CLINICAL fixture (must be BUILT, see ground_truth_to_build): ~50-100 pages spanning POLARIS's REAL source types (peer-review journal HTML, FDA label / EMA SmPC, ClinicalTrials.gov, clinical guideline, preprint), table-bearing pages OVERSAMPLED, each hand-labeled with gold main body + gold table trees — this is §-1.1 point 4 (domain-appropriate benchmark), not scope creep.
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NOT a banned proxy. It does NOT count words/citations/sources (the decision is overlap-against-truth, not 'how many'). It does NOT do pattern-presence — junk is defined as n-grams ABSENT FROM THE HUMAN GOLD BODY, never as 'lines matching a boilerplate regex' (that regex-presence definition is explicitly the §-1.1 landmine and is rejected; the regex strip is held fixed, not used as the scorer). It does NOT compare metadata between systems. It is NOT sample-based — every page in the locked list is scored, claim-equivalent here being every page's full body against its full gold. It is NOT a string-presence PASS/FAIL — it is a continuous precision/recall/F1 against a labeled reference. Critically, it CANNOT be computed on unlabeled POLARIS HTML (that would force the banned regex/count definition), which is precisely WHY a labeled clinical fixture must be built before any in-domain score is trusted.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: This layer scores ONLY the quality of the extracted body text vs a labeled reference; it never invokes, reads, modifies, or relaxes the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) — those are not in the data path of this isolation test at all. Faithfulness is in fact STRENGTHENED, not touched: every content-of-record candidate is required to be EXTRACTIVE/verbatim (Trafilatura, Resiliparse, jusText, union, MinerU-HTML all preserve source wording — MinerU verified extractive in the Dripper paper: emits DOM-block labels, reconstructs from the original DOM, 'eliminates format hallucinations'), so the spans strict_verify later checks are byte-identical to the fetched source. The generative ReaderLM-v2 is benched as a yardstick but is STRUCTURALLY barred from being crowned as content-of-record precisely because rewriting a span would break the verifier's span match — the metric's tie-break and the never-crown rule enforce that, so a better ROUGE number can never buy a faithfulness regression.

specs.gate0_canary: MANDATORY before any candidate is trusted (anti-drb_72). THREE checks, all must pass or the harness FAILS LOUD (non-zero exit, no scores emitted): (1) POSITIVE deterministic canary — feed the GOLD main body itself in as the 'extraction'; the official WebMainBench scorer MUST return F1 ~= 1.0 (>= 0.99). Proves the scorer rewards perfect extraction. (2) NEGATIVE deterministic canary (the drb_72 'garbage that looks real' guard) — hand-build a fixture page = pure nav/ads/cookie-banner/SEO boilerplate with NONE of the gold main body; the scorer MUST return F1 ~= 0 (<= 0.05). Proves the scorer scores junk LOW — drb_72 was a high score on garbage, so this is the load-bearing check. (3) PUBLISHED-NUMBER reproduction — run the OFFICIAL scorer (ROUGE-N N=5 jieba, exact per-extractor variant: Trafilatura Html+MD, Resiliparse TEXT) on the WebMainBench split and reproduce the Dripper Table-2 anchors within a stated tolerance band: Trafilatura ~=0.6402, Resiliparse ~=0.6290, Readability ~=0.6543, jusText(en) ~=0.4783, MinerU-HTML ~=0.8779 (each +/- tolerance). A config mismatch (wrong N, wrong tokenizer, wrong variant, wrong split) shows up here as an anchor miss and is treated as a HARNESS BUG, not an extractor result — exactly the wiring-bug class drb_72 hid. Lineage sha256 manifest (scorer config + tokenizer + split + gold rev + each extractor id/version) is pinned and printed; harness is GREEN only when (1)+(2)+(3) all pass.
specs.ground_truth_to_build: PARTIAL reuse + REQUIRED build. REUSE for the general yardstick + GATE-0 anchors: WebMainBench (opendatalab, ~7,809 labeled pages, gold Markdown, official ROUGE-N scorer; gives the published per-extractor numbers for the GATE-0 reproduction check) — no build needed, filter to biomedical/scientific domains where the field allows. BUILD (needs_fixture, the clinical-decision relevance the general set cannot cover): a small clinical extraction fixture of ~50-100 real pages spanning POLARIS's actual source types (peer-review journal HTML, FDA label / EMA SmPC, ClinicalTrials.gov record, clinical guideline, preprint), with table-bearing pages OVERSAMPLED, each hand-labeled with (a) gold main body Markdown and (b) gold table trees (for TEDS). Rationale: WebMainBench is general/multilingual and ROUGE n-gram overlap is blind to table row/col structure — yet MinerU's whole reason to be a candidate is clinical table fidelity (TEDS 0.739 vs Trafilatura 0.341). Without this fixture the clinical table case is invisible to the bench, so the clinical-relevance verdict for every candidate is honestly marked needs_fixture until it is built.
specs.notes: All ids/licenses primary-source verified 2026-06-23: MinerU-HTML opendatalab/MinerU-HTML v1.1 base Qwen3-0.6B, Apache-2.0 weights AND code (HF README), CONFIRMED EXTRACTIVE/verbatim from Dripper arXiv 2511.23119 (DOM-block label-and-reconstruct, no paraphrase) → safe as content-of-record. ReaderLM-v2 is CC-BY-NC-4.0 + generative → yardstick-only, structurally never crowned (demonstrates the §-1.1 anti-gaming guard). Published WebMainBench anchors (Dripper Table 2) double as deterministic GATE-0 reproduction targets. Mirrors I-qgen-001 structure: SCREEN(advisory)/DECIDE(isolation), locked page list + paired bootstrap + Holm/FDR + min-effect, finalists rerun >=3x. Honest open items: (a) confirm WebMainBench ships an official eval script (if not, GATE-0 reproduction uses the paper's published config to re-derive — still anchored, just re-implemented and tolerance-checked against the published numbers); (b) verify resiliparse extras vs core pip name (chatnoir-resiliparse) at build time; (c) the clinical fixture build is the gating dependency before any in-domain (vs general-yardstick) decision is trusted. Faithfulness engine untouched throughout; recall is a hard non-regression floor vs the Trafilatura incumbent (weight-not-filter, §-1.3); the only hard drop at this layer remains the obvious-junk structural floor, unchanged. Relevant repo files for the build brief: src/tools/access_bypass.py (safe_trafilatura_extract:943, clean_fetch_body:1661, strip_web_boilerplate:1412); docs/retrieval_landscape_2026.md §3/§5/§6.

specs.holding_condition: IterResearch/Tongyi queries are FIXED (the I-qgen-001 winner, PG_QGEN_ITERRESEARCH default), and the ENTIRE rest of the retrieval stack stays at current POLARIS default — Serper discovery, crawl4ai+Zyte fetch, Trafilatura extraction, clean_fetch_body line-strip, ContentDeduplicator near-dup, tier_classifier + authority_model weighting, no reranker. NO retrieval is re-run: the fixture is drawn from a banked corpus_snapshot.json produced under exactly these fixed defaults, so 'queries fixed' is satisfied by construction. The quality_weight model sits at landscape step 7 (after extraction/strip/dedup), so EVERY candidate scores the IDENTICAL post-extraction, post-clean_fetch_body, post-dedup body text for each source — only the scoring model varies. AUC is rank-based, so the heterogeneous candidate score scales (fastText hq-probability vs 0–5 edu regression vs taxonomy category index) need no normalization for the cross-candidate comparison. The output is consumed ONLY as a per-source WEIGHT into the existing authority_model blend; in the bake-off it is NEVER used as a drop/filter — no threshold is applied to remove a source.

candidates.name: DCLM fastText classifier (OH-2.5 + ELI5)
candidates.impl_notes: fastText .bin; load with `import fasttext; m=fasttext.load_model('model.bin')`. REQUIRES numpy<2.0. Output __label__hq probability = the weight (rank by P(hq)). Normalize/lowercase text, strip newlines, score the post-extraction body. Pure CPU, fastest candidate.

candidates.name: Ultra-FineWeb verification-loop fastText
candidates.impl_notes: fastText, released 2025-06-16. Run via repo `infer_single_content.py` (single) or `infer_folder.py` (batch). REQUIRES numpy<2.0 per model card. Output = Pred label + Pred score; rank by Pred score (P of high-quality). 99x cheaper verification loop than DCLM per paper; CPU-only.

candidates.name: Essential-Web EAI-Distill-0.5b taxonomy annotator
candidates.license: UNDER-SPECIFIED on card (HTTP 401) — base is Qwen2.5-0.5B (Apache-2.0); VERIFY-BEFORE-ADOPT
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: ~2-3 GB (0.5B params, bf16; fits any single GPU, can also run CPU slowly)
candidates.impl_notes: Generative SLM distilled from Qwen2.5-32B; emits a 12-field taxonomy (arXiv 2506.14111), NOT a scalar. For the weight use the document-quality / content-complexity / reasoning-depth axis (e.g. the 'reasoning depth' / 'technical correctness' field) as an ordinal weight — NOT the Free-Decimal-Correspondence (FDC) field, which is TOPIC and would leak relevance into this layer. Load via transformers AutoModelForCausalLM; parse the structured label output. GGUF mirror QuantFactory/eai-distill-0.5b-GGUF available for CPU.

candidates.name: FineWeb-Edu classifier
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: ~1-2 GB (Snowflake-arctic-embed-m BERT + linear regression head; trivial on any GPU)
candidates.impl_notes: BERT-like regression head on Snowflake-arctic-embed-m; outputs a continuous 0–5 educational-value score = the weight (rank by score). Load via transformers AutoModelForSequenceClassification, max 512 tokens — score the lead body span. Educational value is a quality PROXY, not clinical authority (honest caveat). CPU-runnable but GPU is faster for the full fixture.

candidates.name: Nemotron-CC ensemble (Nemotron-4 + Mixtral edu + DCLM)
candidates.license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Apache-2.0 (verified on Nemotron-4 card) — VERIFY commercial-use clause before adopt
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: ~3-4 GB total (two arctic-embed-m BERT heads on GPU + one CPU fastText)
candidates.impl_notes: Per Nemotron-CC paper the ensemble = mean of the per-model normalized 0–5 edu scores (the two NVIDIA arctic-embed heads) fused with DCLM fastText; specify mean-of-normalized as the fusion. Both NVIDIA heads = AutoModelForSequenceClassification, 512-tok. The +5.6 MMLU-over-DCLM result motivates testing it; the weight = the fused score.

candidates.name: POLARIS heuristic score_content_quality (current floor)
candidates.impl_notes: The incumbent. Re-purposed here as a RANKER (use the returned 0.0–1.0 score as the weight), NOT as the document-gate drop it ships as — the gate stays default-OFF. Answers the load-bearing question: does any learned classifier actually beat the heuristic POLARIS already has? Run it on the identical post-extraction body.

candidates.name: Constant / random weight
candidates.impl_notes: Sanity floor: a constant or uniform-random weight must score AUC ~0.5. Any candidate not clearly above this is signal-free. Doubles as half of the GATE-0 metric canary (tied scores -> AUC 0.5).

candidates.name: LLM-judge quality score (GLM-5.2)
candidates.impl_id: GLM-5.2 via OpenRouter (z-ai/glm-5.1 per runtime lock legacy_compat / GLM-5.2 campaign backbone)
candidates.impl_notes: Prompt the backbone to emit a 0–1 source-quality score; rank by it. Included as the informative CEILING: landscape §7 reports GPT-5 kappa~=0 on AI-slop detection, so a high LLM ceiling would be surprising and a low one confirms automatic quality detection is immature. NOT a production pick (LLM-in-extraction-loop is a faithfulness hazard); yardstick only.

metric.name: Quality-weight ranking AUC on an on-topic clinical authoritative-vs-SEO-spam fixture
metric.definition: For each candidate, score every fixture source -> a scalar weight, then compute ROC-AUC of that weight against the binary human label (1=authoritative, 0=on-topic SEO-spam/shallow), grouping by clinical topic so authoritative and spam items are paired WITHIN the same subject (the score must separate quality, not topic). PRIMARY = ROC-AUC; SECONDARY = average precision / PR-AUC (robustness when spam is the rare class). Recall-preservation GUARD, expressed without any drop: fraction of human-labeled T1/T2/T3 authoritative sources that land in the bottom weight-decile must stay near zero (directly tests 'does the weight demote a real authoritative source' while never filtering). Winner = highest ROC-AUC with the guard near zero. The weight is NEVER thresholded to remove a source anywhere in the test.
metric.ground_truth_source: A purpose-built labeled fixture (see ground_truth_to_build): clinical sources drawn from the banked corpus_snapshot.json (fixed-default IterResearch retrieval), each HUMAN dual-annotated authoritative vs on-topic-spam with inter-annotator kappa reported. Labels are produced INDEPENDENTLY of all five candidate classifiers AND independently of tier_classifier/authority_model — using human judgement against the cited body text — so the ground truth is not circular (avoids the drb_72 fake-real-number trap). Anchor reference for label definitions: DRB-II / §-1.1 source-credibility rubrics for what 'authoritative' means in a clinical corpus.
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NOT a count, pattern-presence, metadata comparison, or string-presence PASS/FAIL. It scores REAL post-extraction corpus body text against an INDEPENDENT human ground-truth of source quality, measuring whether the learned weight RANKS authoritative-above-spam (a genuine quality judgement on real content), not 'how many sources' or 'does the text contain token X'. It is whole-fixture, not sample-based (every fixture item scored). The labels come from human line-by-line reading of the cited content, the §-1.1 standard itself.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: ROC-AUC integrates over ALL thresholds without ever committing to a drop point — it is the formal statement of weight-not-filter, so by construction this metric cannot be used as a hard filter. The layer's output is consumed only as a per-source WEIGHT surfaced to consolidation/authority_model; the obvious-junk structural floor (junk_detection.py) is untouched and remains the only hard drop. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / span-grounding / NLI / 4-role / provenance) is never invoked, read, or modified by this bake-off — it operates downstream on whatever sources survive, identical regardless of which quality-weight model wins. No span text is rewritten (all candidates are scorers, not extractors), so the verbatim spans the verifier later checks are byte-identical across candidates.

specs.gate0_canary: TWO halves, both must pass before any candidate AUC is trusted. (A) METRIC canary: feed the AUC scorer three synthetic (label, score) sets with KNOWN-correct answers — perfectly-ranked -> AUC must equal exactly 1.0; perfectly-inverted -> exactly 0.0; all-tied/constant -> exactly 0.5. If any deviates, the harness is mis-wired (the drb_72 'fake numbers that look real' failure) and NO score is trusted. (B) MODEL-WIRING canary (the half a metric-only canary misses): for EACH loaded candidate, feed one obviously-authoritative doc (e.g. an FDA label / Cochrane review body) and one obvious-garbage doc (cookie-banner + keyword-stuffed spam) and ASSERT the model ranks authoritative strictly above garbage AND that the two scores DIFFER (not a constant) — this proves the classifier actually loaded and emits real varying signal rather than silently returning a stub/constant that would still produce a real-looking AUC. A candidate failing (B) is excluded as non-functional, not scored.
specs.ground_truth_to_build: YES — build a labeled fixture first. Name: clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl. Size: ~300-500 sources (target >=150 authoritative + >=150 on-topic-spam) drawn from the banked corpus_snapshot.json, grouped into ~15-25 clinical topics so each topic contains BOTH authoritative and on-topic-spam items (forces the metric to separate quality, not topic). CRITICAL: both classes must be ON-TOPIC to the same clinical questions, varying only quality/authority — a 'good clinical vs random web junk' fixture would measure topic relevance (the reranker layer's job), not quality. Each item = {url, post_extraction_body, topic_id, label, annotator_notes}. Dual human annotation, inter-annotator kappa reported, labels independent of all five candidates and of tier_classifier/authority_model. Zero-shot scoring note: all five listed candidates are pretrained and scored zero-shot, so the entire fixture is a clean held-out test set (apples-to-apples). IF a re-seeded clinical fastText (landscape §6 ADD-1) is later added, it must get a separate train split and be scored only on held-out items, and the fixture sized up accordingly — flagged so this is never left ambiguous.
specs.notes: Scope honesty: fastText candidates (DCLM, Ultra-FineWeb) are CPU 'yes'; the BERT/SLM/ensemble candidates are 'needs_gpu' (all tiny, <4 GB); the LLM-judge yardstick is 'no_key'. ALL candidates are equally blocked on the fixture — runnable reflects per-candidate compute reality, the fixture is the global ground_truth_to_build (more informative than collapsing all to needs_fixture). HONEST interpretation caveat: DCLM / FineWeb-Edu / Nemotron-CC measure EDUCATIONAL value — a quality PROXY, not clinical authority (a terse FDA label may score LOW on 'educational' while being maximally authoritative). The bake-off honestly tests whether a general web-quality prior TRANSFERS to clinical-authority ranking; a mediocre winner is a valid finding and may argue for the re-seeded clinical fastText (§6 ADD-1) instead. Automatic AI-slop/SEO-spam detection is genuinely immature (landscape §7: AUPRC 0.52-0.55, GPT-5 kappa~=0) — hence the LLM-judge ceiling yardstick and the rule that the winner is consumed only as a LOW-WEIGHT down-rank, never a gate. License verify-before-adopt: EssentialAI taxonomy card 401'd (base Qwen2.5-0.5B is Apache-2.0; full fine-tune license unconfirmed) and Nemotron-4 carries the NVIDIA Open Model License alongside Apache-2.0 — both must be license-cleared before any production port (sovereignty). The winner is ported into the existing authority_model blend (authority_score = clamp01(sum_signal(w*score)) * junk_cap * corroboration) as a new learned-content-quality signal weight; the standalone AUC SELECTS, the final combined integrated run DECIDES.

specs.layer: dedup (near-duplicate collapse on extracted source bodies, run BEFORE basket weight / consolidation)
specs.holding_condition: IterResearch/Tongyi queries are held FIXED (the I-qgen-001 winner, flag PG_QGEN_ITERRESEARCH). The rest of the retrieval stack is at current POLARIS default and is used only to PRODUCE the held-fixed shared corpus: Serper search → crawl4ai/Zyte fetch → Trafilatura extract → clean_fetch_body line-level strip (access_bypass.py). The SAME extracted+cleaned bodies (the `evidence_for_gen` records of a banked corpus_snapshot.json) feed EVERY dedup candidate — no candidate re-retrieves or re-extracts. Downstream is NOT run: basket-weight / consolidation (finding_dedup/fact_dedup), generator (multi_section), verify (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance), render (report.md/bibliography.json) are all OFF. We score the candidate's near-dup CLUSTERING directly against per-pair gold labels. The 'dedup runs before basket weight so corroboration is not double-counted' requirement is verified STRUCTURALLY (the dedup hop sits pre-consolidation in the pipeline order) — NOT re-measured downstream, because measuring it downstream would break isolation. Only the dedup mechanism varies.

candidates.name: POLARIS incumbent ContentDeduplicator (MinHash-128 + SimHash-64, O(N^2) pairwise, keep_first DROP)
candidates.impl_id: src/utils/content_deduplicator.py (in-repo; stdlib hashlib only, no extra dep)
candidates.impl_notes: THE FLOOR per standard-process step 2. Instantiate ContentDeduplicator(DeduplicationConfig(near_duplicate_threshold=0.85)); call .deduplicate(items, content_key) on the snapshot evidence bodies; read result.clusters / result.duplicates for the merged pairs to score. NOTE its current keep_first behavior DROPS the duplicate (collision with consolidate-keep-all DNA) — for the ISOLATION metric we only score its clustering decisions (which pairs it merges), not its drop side-effect. Its `deduplication_ratio` (line 132) is the BANNED count and is NOT the metric. O(N^2) over ~500 bodies is fine offline.

candidates.name: SimHash standalone (64-bit, Hamming-distance threshold)
candidates.impl_id: src/utils/content_deduplicator.py::_compute_simhash + _simhash_distance (isolate the SimHash path; or `simhash` PyPI 1.11 as cross-check)
candidates.impl_notes: Dated reference baseline per landscape sec.10 (SimHash 2002, MinHash+LSH beats it for text). Bench it standalone with a swept Hamming-distance threshold so the bake-off shows WHY the field moved off it. Reuse the in-repo _compute_simhash/_simhash_distance to avoid a new dep; cluster by Hamming distance <= k over the 64-bit fingerprints.

candidates.name: MinHash + LSH (datasketch MinHashLSH, real LSH index + threshold sweep)
candidates.impl_id: pip install datasketch==1.6.5 ; datasketch.MinHash + datasketch.MinHashLSH (alt pipeline wrapper: pip install text-dedup, Apache-2.0)
candidates.impl_notes: The 2025 production-standard dedup (FineWeb2/RedPajama-V2/Olmo3 all run it). Tokenize each cleaned body into word n-gram shingles (n=3..5 sweep), MinHash(num_perm=128..256), insert into MinHashLSH(threshold=0.7..0.9 sweep), query each doc for its near-dup bucket → connected components = clusters. Score precision/recall of those clusters vs gold. Sweep (num_perm, shingle_n, threshold) and report the operating point that maximizes recall subject to the precision floor (point 4).

candidates.name: SemHash + Model2Vec static embedder (semantic near-dup via potion-base-8M)
candidates.impl_id: pip install semhash ; from semhash import SemHash ; default encoder minishlab/potion-base-8M (Model2Vec static), Vicinity ANN backend
candidates.license: semhash: MIT ; model2vec: MIT ; minishlab/potion-base-8M weights: MIT
candidates.gpu_gb_estimate: 0 (static lookup-table embedder, CPU; ~30MB weights downloaded from HF, no GPU)
candidates.impl_notes: SemHash.from_records(records).self_deduplicate() returns a DeduplicationResult whose .selected/.filtered + per-record duplicate clusters (with similarity) are readable for scoring (verified via repo docs). Pin the encoder explicitly to minishlab/potion-base-8M for determinism. THIS is the candidate that can WIN on spun/paraphrased syndication (byte/shingle methods miss spun copies) but is also the one MOST at risk of FALSE-MERGING two distinct independent sources that paraphrase the same claim — i.e. the faithfulness/precision arm (stratum a of the fixture) is precisely where it must be stress-tested. Sweep its similarity threshold.

metric.name: Pairwise near-dup collapse Precision/Recall against per-pair gold labels (recall maximized subject to a precision floor)
metric.definition: On a labeled near-dup fixture, enumerate EVERY unordered pair (i,j) of source bodies (exhaustive over all C(N,2) pairs — not a sample). Each pair has a gold label gold(i,j) in {syndicated_copy, distinct} from the dual-annotated fixture. Each candidate produces clusters; system(i,j)=merged iff i,j land in the same cluster. Then: RECALL = (#pairs gold=syndicated_copy AND system=merged) / (#pairs gold=syndicated_copy)  [collapses real duplicates]; PRECISION = (#pairs gold=syndicated_copy AND system=merged) / (#pairs system=merged)  [does NOT merge distinct sources]. FALSE-MERGE-RATE = (#pairs gold=distinct AND system=merged) / (#pairs gold=distinct) — the faithfulness arm, reported separately on the hard-negative stratum (distinct sources that paraphrase the SAME claim). DECISION RULE is asymmetric (a false-merge destroys an independent corroborator; a missed dup merely fails to consolidate): WINNER = max RECALL on the syndicated stratum SUBJECT TO false-merges-of-distinct-same-claim-pairs near zero (precision floor, e.g. FALSE-MERGE-RATE <= 0.01). Raw F1 is explicitly NOT the selector. Sweep each candidate's threshold(s) and report its best feasible operating point.
metric.ground_truth_source: A labeled near-dup fixture BUILT from the banked, held-fixed real corpus snapshots already on disk (state/reserved_corpus_snapshots/*.json and outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/*/corpus_snapshot.json — 6 snapshots, each ~500 `evidence_for_gen` records carrying source_url + direct_quote/statement body + tier + doi/pmid + title). These already contain the real syndication distribution a deep-research crawl produces: one press release reposted across news sites; one journal abstract mirrored on PubMed + publisher + ResearchGate. Per-pair labels are DUAL-ANNOTATED by Claude AND Codex independently with adjudication of disagreements (operationalizes the sec.-1.1 parallel-audit rule on the ground truth itself), each pair labeled syndicated_copy vs distinct with a quoted-span justification.
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NOT a count-as-quality proxy: the score is binary-classification accuracy of merge decisions ANCHORED TO A PER-PAIR HUMAN/DUAL-AI GOLD LABEL, the textbook dedup metric — not 'how many sources survived'. It explicitly does NOT use the incumbent's deduplication_ratio = 1 - unique/original (content_deduplicator.py:132), which IS the banned source-count metric. It is NOT pattern-presence (no 'does the body contain string X'), NOT metadata comparison, and NOT sample-based: precision/recall are computed EXHAUSTIVELY over every labeled pair in the fixture, not over a sampled subset. The unit scored is a real merge decision on real extracted body text against a cited-span gold label.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: This layer never touches strict_verify / NLI entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding — those engines are OFF in the isolation test and unchanged in the pipeline. Dedup is upstream of consolidation and the verifier; it only collapses byte/semantic near-copies of the SAME body so corroboration is not double-counted in basket weight. The PRECISION / FALSE-MERGE-RATE arm is itself a faithfulness guard: it FAILS any candidate that merges two DISTINCT independent sources carrying the same claim (which would silently delete a real corroborator, violating sec.-1.3 consolidate-keep-all). A dedup candidate can never drop a distinct claim and can never relax the verifier; the only hard drop in the stack remains the obvious-junk structural floor, which is a different layer.

specs.gate0_canary: BIDIRECTIONAL canary (the drb_72 'fake numbers that look real' defense) — the harness must score UP for good behavior AND DOWN for known-bad, on 4 fixed micro-inputs, BEFORE any candidate is trusted: (POSITIVE-1) two byte-identical bodies -> harness MUST report that pair recall=1.0 (clustered). (POSITIVE-2) two obviously-distinct bodies (AI-labor vs Parkinsons-DBS, pulled from two different snapshots) -> harness MUST report precision=1.0 on them (NOT clustered, no false merge). (NEGATIVE-1, no-op control) feed a stub dedup that clusters NOTHING -> harness MUST report RECALL=0.0 on the syndicated stratum (if it reports high recall, the scorer is hallucinating merges — STOP). (NEGATIVE-2, over-merge control) feed a stub dedup that clusters EVERYTHING into one cluster -> harness MUST report PRECISION ~ base-rate and FALSE-MERGE-RATE=1.0 (if it reports high precision, the scorer is not actually checking the distinct-pair labels — STOP). Only when all four hold (good->up, no-op->recall floor, over-merge->precision/false-merge floor) is the harness trusted and the bake-off allowed to run. This proves the harness reads the gold labels and computes both arms correctly, not just that it can emit a plausible 1.0.
specs.ground_truth_to_build: BUILD a labeled near-dup pair fixture (the snapshots exist but carry NO near-dup pair labels). Source: the 6 banked held-fixed corpus snapshots (state/reserved_corpus_snapshots/ + outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/), ~500 evidence bodies each. Size/composition: from these, assemble ~300-500 source bodies into a fixture with TWO adversarial discriminating strata (without them every candidate scores ~1.0 and the bake-off is theater): (a) HARD NEGATIVES — pairs of DISTINCT independent sources that paraphrase the SAME claim (different URL/author/outlet, same finding), which MUST NOT merge — this is the faithfulness/precision arm and where SemHash semantic similarity is most likely to wrongly merge a real corroborator; (b) TRUE POSITIVES — spun/syndicated copies of one body (press release reposted across outlets; abstract mirrored on PubMed/publisher/ResearchGate; light-rewrite spam), which MUST merge — where byte-exact misses and MinHash/semantic catch. Plus easy controls (byte-identical, fully-unrelated). Per-pair labels DUAL-ANNOTATED Claude+Codex independently with adjudication, each label carrying the cited-span justification. Persist under tests/fixtures/ (per LAW VI). Estimated build: ~0.5 day of dual-annotation + adjudication; no GPU, no re-retrieval.
specs.notes: All four candidates are CPU-only (0 GPU): MinHash/SimHash are stdlib hashing; SemHash's potion-base-8M is a Model2Vec STATIC lookup-table embedder (~30MB, no GPU). All licenses are MIT/Apache-2.0 — fully sovereign — so there is NO yardstick_non_sovereign row for this layer (honest: none exists; I did not invent one). The incumbent ContentDeduplicator is the role=baseline floor per standard-process step 2. PORTING NOTE (not part of the isolation metric, for the later wire-in diff): the winning mechanism must be wired in CONSOLIDATE-keep-all-in-basket mode (group same-body copies, keep all source_urls as multi-citation, collapse only the corroboration WEIGHT), NOT the incumbent's keep_first DROP — per sec.-1.3 'consolidate, don't drop'. The faithfulness engine is untouched throughout.

metric.name: Credibility-graded NDCG@K + required-source recall@K guard + off-topic-drop precision (triplet)
metric.definition: On the labeled candidate pool for each IterResearch query, three facets scored together (K = the current POLARIS fetch cap). (1) JUNK-REDUCTION — credibility-GRADED-gain NDCG@K and MRR: each candidate carries an ordinal gain credible-relevant(3) > low-credibility-relevant(2) > on-topic-spam(1) > off-topic(0); NDCG@K rewards a reranker that puts credible-relevant rows above on-topic-spam above off-topic. Graded (never a binary cut) is exactly where weight-not-filter lives in the metric. (2) RECALL-PRESERVATION GUARD — required-source recall@K = fraction of DRB-II info_recall-bearing sources still present in top-K after rerank; this MUST NOT regress vs the lexical floor. Catches an aggressive reranker that wins NDCG while burying a required source below the fetch cap (the over-drop / weight-not-filter violation). (3) DROP SUB-METRIC — on the OFF-TOPIC half ONLY, at the calibrated drop-threshold: off-topic-drop precision/recall = must drop off-topic items, must NOT drop on-topic-spam or credible-relevant (those are DEMOTED by low gain, never dropped — the only hard drop is the obvious-junk floor, which is a different layer). Winner = highest graded-NDCG@K AND non-regressing required-source recall@K AND clean off-topic-drop precision. No facet is a count of sources.
metric.ground_truth_source: RELEVANCE half: DRB-II info_recall rubrics (third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl) — a candidate is relevance-positive iff it supplies a required info_recall point (same legitimacy as the I-qgen-001 coverage harness), with the blocked-reference excluded via load_blocked_references. CREDIBILITY half: an INDEPENDENT authority-rubric annotation (judge + authority rubric, Codex/human cross-audited per §-1.1.5) — deliberately NOT POLARIS's own tier/authority_score field, because grading the pipeline by its own metadata is the banned 'metadata comparison'. ON-TOPIC-SPAM + OFF-TOPIC negatives: labeled exemplars injected into the fixture (the natural snapshot pool has them removed/under-represented), each tagged with its gain class. Frozen as a versioned fixture so every reranker is scored against identical labels.
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NDCG/MRR over per-candidate ground-truth RANKING LABELS, not a count. Relevance labels come from DRB-II info_recall (claim-grounded, the same source §-1.1 already accepts for coverage); credibility labels are annotated INDEPENDENTLY of POLARIS's own tier field (so it is not the banned tier-vs-tier metadata comparison). It is not word/citation/source counting ('how many sources' is explicitly rejected — the score is positional quality of the ranking), not pattern-presence ('does it mention X'), not a string-presence PASS/FAIL, and not sample-based (every candidate in the frozen pool for the gold idxs is labeled and scored). The graded gain encodes credibility as a WEIGHT (ordinal gain), never a hard filter, satisfying §-1.3.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: The reranker only re-orders and selects top-K of the frozen candidate pool BEFORE the faithfulness engine ever runs; this isolation test never invokes strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance, never relaxes any threshold, and produces no report. The metric scores ranking quality against external labels, not faithfulness — so it cannot trade faithfulness for a higher number. The required-source recall@K guard further ensures rerank cannot silently drop evidence the verifier would later need (no over-drop), and credibility is a demotion-weight not a drop, so a credible-relevant source is never removed by this layer.

specs.gate0_canary: TWO-PART canary; no reranker is trusted until BOTH pass. PART A (lineage, reuse gate0_lineage.py): assert the labeled fixture is bound to the canonical DRB-II idx (load_canonical_question / SLUG_TO_IDX), info_recall points loaded from the gold file, blocked-reference excluded — so relevance labels are scored against the RIGHT rubric (the drb_72 wrong-question fix). PART B (scorer-math, the drb_72 'fake numbers that look real' gate — the part the task mandates beyond lineage): hand-build a ~6-item labeled mini-fixture with gains, e.g. [credible-relevant=3, credible-relevant=3, low-cred-relevant=2, on-topic-spam=1, off-topic=0, off-topic=0]; (i) feed ONE fixed permutation whose NDCG@K was computed BY HAND and assert harness output == that hand value (bit-for-bit, fail loud on mismatch); (ii) assert perfect ideal ordering -> NDCG@K == 1.0 and fully-reversed ordering -> the known-low hand value; (iii) assert MRR matches a hand value for a known first-relevant position; (iv) threshold check: assert the calibrated drop-threshold drops the two OFF-TOPIC items but does NOT drop the on-topic-spam(1) or any credible-relevant(3) item; (v) recall-guard check: assert required-source recall@K computes correctly when a known required source is pushed below K. 'The harness runs' is NOT the canary — the canary is 'the harness reproduces a hand-computed score and a hand-specified drop decision.'
specs.ground_truth_to_build: BUILD a labeled relevance+credibility candidate-pool fixture (the frozen pool can be EXTRACTED from banked corpus_snapshot.json retrieval.classified_sources/evidence_rows for the gold idxs, but it must then be LABELED — it cannot be reused as-is). Why not reuse: evidence_for_gen is POST-selection (junk already removed) and the natural pool's tier field is POLARIS self-grading, so neither can discriminate rerankers. Steps: (1) take the pre-rerank pool (~849 classified_sources / ~797 evidence_rows per idx) from the 4 gold-bearing snapshots (drb_72/75/76/78); (2) relevance-label each candidate via DRB-II info_recall (does it supply a required point); (3) credibility-label each candidate via an INDEPENDENT authority rubric, judge-annotated + Codex/human cross-audited (NOT the tier field); (4) INJECT and label on-topic-SEO-spam exemplars + off-topic exemplars (the natural pool under-represents pure on-topic spam — it dies at the SERP/off-topic filter — so the discriminating negatives must be added); (5) freeze as a versioned fixture with per-candidate gain in {0,1,2,3}. Size: ~4 gold idxs x ~800-1000 labeled candidates + ~10-20 injected spam/off-topic exemplars per idx. Every model-based candidate is runnable=needs_gpu AND its scoring is needs_fixture (depends on this fixture); the lexical floor is runnable=yes but still scored against the same fixture.
specs.notes: Repo ids verified live (2026-06-23): Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-4B & -8B (apache-2.0), Alibaba-NLP/gte-reranker-modernbert-base (149M, apache-2.0), mixedbread-ai/mxbai-rerank-base-v2 (apache-2.0), zeroentropy/zerank-1-small (apache-2.0 — only zerank-2 is CC-BY-NC), BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3 (apache-2.0, dated baseline). nvidia/llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2 is NVIDIA Open Model License (NOT Apache) -> yardstick_non_sovereign, never selectable as winner (sovereignty). Faithfulness gates untouched throughout.
