metric.name: Dual-axis isolation relevance score: (A) AUC(on-topic > off-topic) separation + (B) reasoning-retrieval recall@k on non-lexical evidence
metric.definition: Per banked question, over the FIXED candidate set in that question's corpus_snapshot.json: AXIS A (separation, drives single-vector + late-interaction) — score every source with the candidate (single-vector: query↔body cosine; late-interaction: query↔body MaxSim), then compute AUC = P(score(on-topic source) > score(off-topic source)) across all POS×NEG pairs from the hand-checked labeled set. AUC=1.0 means every on-topic source ranks above every off-topic contaminant; AUC<0.5 is the rank-inversion failure I-arch-009 found. A candidate is admissible iff AUC>0.5 on EVERY question AND no regression vs the all-MiniLM floor on drb_76/drb_78 (the two where whitening hurt). AXIS B (reasoning retrieval, the late-interaction CLAIMED EDGE) — on a small per-question fixture of expert-rubric-required claims whose supporting corpus source does NOT lexically overlap the IterResearch query (a reasoning hop), rank all sources by the candidate and report recall@k (k = the count actually fed to consolidation at POLARIS default) of the gold supporting source(s). Winner per axis is the highest mean across questions with the admissibility gate satisfied; the late-interaction layer is adopted ONLY if it wins Axis B materially over the best single-vector (its purpose-built regime), else the single-vector embedder is the pick and late-interaction is shelved. Borderline rows (scores within a small band of the POS/NEG boundary) are PRINTED and read by eye (§-1.1), never regex-trusted.
metric.ground_truth_source: AXIS A: the hand-checkable LABEL_SETS already banked in scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py (per-question POSITIVE = must-hit-both-facet-lists on-topic keywords, NEGATIVE = the specific off-topic content that contaminated THAT corpus, e.g. drb_72 ai_labor POS=AI∧labor, NEG=ptsd/colorectal/parkinson) — drawn from the REAL banked corpora, counts+samples printed for eye-verification. AXIS B: a NEW small labeled fixture (see ground_truth_to_build) derived from the DRB-II expert rubrics for those 5 questions — each gold (claim → supporting source) pair hand-verified to be a genuine reasoning hop (the source supports the rubric point without lexically matching the query).
metric.section_1_1_compliant_why: It is NOT a count and NOT a pattern-presence/metadata proxy. AUC is a per-pair ordering of REAL corpus sources by whether on-topic content out-ranks the actual off-topic contamination in that corpus — it measures corpus relevance QUALITY (does the scorer keep the right sources high and the junk low), exactly the junk-reduction/recall-preservation the layer controls. It is not 'how many sources' (banned), not 'does the report mention X' (banned), not a string-presence PASS/FAIL gate (banned), not a sample-based audit (every POS×NEG pair is scored, and borderline rows are read individually per §-1.1). Recall@k of gold reasoning-hop evidence is recall-PRESERVATION on real ground truth, not a citation tally. No metadata comparison between systems — each candidate is scored on the same real corpus against the same hand-checked labels.
metric.faithfulness_safe_why: This layer scores relevance to ORDER/WEIGHT sources for consolidation; it never reads, gates, or alters the faithfulness engine. strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance / span-grounding run unchanged downstream on whatever the consolidation receives. The relevance score is surfaced as a WEIGHT (weight-not-filter §-1.3), never a hard drop — the only hard drop is the unchanged obvious-junk structural floor. A better embedder can only raise the on-topic mass entering an UNCHANGED verifier; it cannot relax any faithfulness threshold, cannot let an unsupported claim pass, and touches no span the verifier later checks (no LLM rewrite of any source body — all candidates are encoders/late-interaction scorers, not generators).

specs.gate0_canary: MANDATORY pre-candidate harness-validity proof, two synthetic checks the AUC/MaxSim harness MUST pass BEFORE any candidate score is trusted (kills the drb_72 'fake numbers that look real' failure): (CANARY-1, known-correct) feed a TRIVIAL labeled set — POS = 5 strings that are pure on-topic for a question (verbatim from its POS facets), NEG = 5 strings that are pure off-topic (verbatim from its NEG list) — through the FULL scoring+AUC code path; a competent scorer MUST return AUC=1.0 (perfect separation). If the harness returns anything other than ~1.0 on an unambiguous input, the harness is wired wrong (split-brain scoring / wrong-query binding / Title-only-stub) and NO candidate is run. (CANARY-2, known-inverted) feed a DELIBERATELY rank-inverted oracle — a fake scorer that returns +1 for NEG and −1 for POS — and assert the harness reports AUC<0.5 (and specifically ~0.0). This proves the AUC computation has the POS/NEG polarity correct and is not silently symmetric. (CANARY-3, identity binding) assert the query the harness scores against == the IterResearch query bound to that snapshot (print both, compare) — prevents the drb_72 wrong-question/packed≠answered defect at this layer. All three print their inputs+outputs for eye-verification (§-1.1) and FAIL LOUD (non-zero exit) on mismatch. Only after all three pass is any real candidate trusted.
specs.ground_truth_to_build: PARTIAL — Axis A reuses existing, Axis B must be built. AXIS A: none to build — reuse scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py LABEL_SETS (5 questions, hand-checkable POS/NEG keyword registry already banked and §-1.1-verified). AXIS B (the late-interaction reasoning-retrieval fixture): BUILD a small labeled set — for each of the 5 banked questions, 5-10 (expert-rubric-required-claim → gold-supporting-source-id-in-snapshot) pairs, selected so the support is a genuine reasoning hop (source does NOT lexically overlap the IterResearch query), each pair hand-verified against the DRB-II rubric and the actual snapshot body span. Size: ~25-50 gold pairs total, hand-checkable in a sitting, stored as tests/fixtures/I-ret-002/reasoning_retrieval_goldset.json with the supporting span quoted inline so a reviewer can eye-confirm the hop. This fixture is REQUIRED before the late-interaction Axis-B claim can be scored; without it, only Axis A (separation) is decidable and the late-interaction candidates cannot be crowned on their claimed edge. Mark candidates needing it: needs_fixture for the Axis-B portion.
specs.notes: Repo ids + licenses web-verified 2026-06-23: lightonai/GTE-ModernColBERT-v1 = Apache-2.0 (note suffix is -v1 not -base), lightonai/Reason-ModernColBERT = CC-BY-NC-4.0, Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B = Apache-2.0, Alibaba-NLP/gte-modernbert-base = Apache-2.0, ibm-granite/granite-embedding-english-r2 = Apache-2.0, google/embeddinggemma-300m = Gemma license (gated, non-sovereign per §9.1.8 no-gemma lock). PyLate = `pip install -U pylate` (github.com/lightonai/pylate). HARNESS REUSE: scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py is the proven I-arch-009 scaffold (AUC-on-real-corpus, single-model-load-then-free, prints borderline rows) — extend it with a PyLate late-interaction branch (MaxSim via pylate rank.rerank over the FIXED snapshot candidate set, not a fresh retrieval) and the GATE-0 canaries; do NOT re-implement. SCOPE HONESTY: this is ISOLATION — no e2e, no render, no judge; the winner is wired flag-gated then validated in the SINGLE combined end-to-end run at the end of the section sequence (per standard_process doc §44-49). all-MiniLM is the explicit FLOOR (must be beaten, not assumed-beaten). Late-interaction adopted ONLY if it wins Axis B materially; otherwise Qwen3-Embedding-8B stands as the I-arch-009 single-vector pick and ColBERT is shelved with the number on record. DECISION RULE for non-sovereign yardsticks (EmbeddingGemma, Reason-ModernColBERT): they bound the ceiling and can justify a sovereign follow-up (e.g. the <2h Apache re-fine-tune of Reason-ModernColBERT) but can NEVER be the production pick.

banned_proxy_violations.why_banned: This is the strongest §-1.3 violation in the design. The metric explicitly REWARDS a hard DROP at a calibrated threshold ('off-topic-drop precision/recall = must drop off-topic items') — that is a new hard filter beyond the obvious-junk structural floor, contradicting both the layer's own claim ('the only hard drop is the obvious-junk floor') and §-1.3 ('everything else is a WEIGHT — never a DROP/CAP/THIN'). Verified against src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:3178 _rerank_and_reserve: the incumbent does a count-cut by score (top-K), NOT a semantic off-topic drop — so the proposed drop-threshold is genuinely NEW behavior the bake-off is optimizing for. A reranker selected for 'clean off-topic-drop precision' is selected to be a better FILTER, which is exactly the weight-not-filter breach the standard exists to catch.
banned_proxy_violations.fix: Drop the off-topic-drop facet entirely OR re-cast it as a pure DEMOTION-weight check (off-topic items get gain 0 and sink in graded-NDCG@K, but are never removed). If an off-topic hard-cut is genuinely wanted it must be a SEPARATE explicitly-justified structural floor with its own §-1.3 sign-off, not folded into the reranker score as a rewarded sub-metric. NDCG@K (facet 1) + required-source recall@K guard (facet 2) already capture all the legitimate signal without any drop.

banned_proxy_violations.metric: Gold-source recall@k against a single canonical gold_url(f) per required finding
banned_proxy_violations.why_banned: Borderline-banned via basket-faithfulness (§-1.3) inversion. The metric scores a backend 1 ONLY if it surfaces the ONE canonical gold_url(f); a backend that surfaces an equally-valid ALTERNATE source carrying the same finding (a publisher mirror not in the normalizer's map, a different primary study supporting the same DRB-II point, a gov restatement) scores 0. That penalizes legitimate corroboration diversity and rewards exact-URL matching — a single-source-truth proxy, the blind spot §-1.3 BASKET FAITHFULNESS names ('never a single URL/span'). It is not a count-of-sources proxy (that part is clean), but the single-URL gold makes it a pattern-match-the-canonical-URL proxy rather than a did-you-discover-a-valid-source-for-the-finding measure.
banned_proxy_violations.fix: Make gold_url(f) a per-finding SOURCE SET (the canonical study DOI PLUS accepted equivalents: known mirrors, the gov/agency restatement, alternative primary sources the DRB-II rubric would accept), and score recall as 'union(ranked URLs) intersects the gold SET'. Build the equivalent-set during fixture construction (Crossref/OpenAlex already give mirror DOIs); fail loud on a finding with zero resolvable sources, never on a finding where a valid alternate exists.

banned_proxy_violations.metric: Quality-weight ranking AUC (authoritative vs on-topic-spam), with topic-grouped pairing
banned_proxy_violations.why_banned: Risk of a 'source-TYPE = better' proxy in disguise — the exact 'more/better sources' trap the task warned about. Pairing is only WITHIN-topic, not within source-TYPE. If authoritative items are predominantly journals/FDA-labels and spam items are predominantly blog/SEO domains, a candidate can win high AUC by learning domain/source-type register (journal-ese vs blog-ese), NOT by judging quality on equal-type content. The educational-value classifiers (DCLM/FineWeb-Edu/Nemotron-CC) are explicitly admitted to measure a register PROXY, which makes this leakage path live. Separately, the recall-preservation guard is defined on 'human-labeled T1/T2/T3 authoritative' — if those tier labels are derived from or anchored to POLARIS's own tier_classifier vocabulary, the guard becomes circular (grading the pipeline by its own metadata, a §-1.1 banned metadata comparison).
banned_proxy_violations.fix: Add within-source-TYPE pairs to the fixture (authoritative AND spam drawn from the SAME register where possible: a sloppy journal-style content-farm vs a real journal; an authoritative .gov page vs a spammy .gov-mimic), and report AUC stratified by source-type so a register-only winner is exposed. State explicitly in the fixture spec that the T1/T2/T3 'authoritative' labels are human judgments of the BODY TEXT, defined independently of tier_classifier output, with the tier vocabulary not shown to annotators — and assert this in the lineage manifest.

banned_proxy_violations.metric: AUC(on-topic > off-topic) over keyword-derived POS/NEG label sets
banned_proxy_violations.why_banned: Mild pattern-presence-as-ground-truth. Verified in scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py label_rows(): POS = all(any(kw in text) for facet) and NEG = any(neg_kw in text) — pure substring matching ('ptsd', 'parkinson', 'AI'∧'labor'). The GOLD LABELS are themselves built by the §-1.1-banned 'does term X appear' construction. The metric over those labels (AUC) is fine, but its ground truth is keyword presence, so a source genuinely on-topic that avoids the keyword is mislabeled NEG, and an off-topic source that happens to contain a keyword is mislabeled POS. The design's mitigation (print borderline rows + eye-verify) tempers but does not remove the dependency on pattern-derived labels.
banned_proxy_violations.fix: Keep the keyword sets as a fast PRE-FILTER for candidate label rows, but require the final POS/NEG gold to be HUMAN-confirmed by reading the body (the eye-verification must be a hard gating step that overrides the keyword label and is recorded per-row), not just a spot-check of borderline cases. Report how many keyword-labeled rows were flipped on human read as a fixture-quality signal.

faithfulness_risks.risk: The off-topic drop-threshold, once ported to production, sits UPSTREAM of strict_verify/NLI/4-role and silently removes evidence the verifier would otherwise see. Unlike a down-weight, a dropped source cannot be recovered into a basket — so an aggressive calibrated threshold can starve the faithfulness engine of a real corroborator (the over-drop failure §-1.3 forbids), even though the isolation test itself never invokes the gates.
faithfulness_risks.fix: Forbid the reranker from removing any non-junk source in production; it may only re-ORDER. If a fetch-cap count-cut is unavoidable, keep the existing count-cut semantics + the required-source recall@K guard as a hard non-regression gate, and never add a semantic drop. Re-validate e2e that post-rerank baskets still contain every verifier-needed source before adoption.

faithfulness_risks.risk: MinerU-HTML and union are asserted extractive/verbatim, but only MinerU is primary-source-confirmed extractive (Dripper paper). The union candidate does near-dup MinHash collapse of three extractors' lines — if any member silently normalizes whitespace/punctuation or re-orders, the surviving 'verbatim' span can drift from source bytes, breaking the byte-identity strict_verify depends on. The faithfulness-safe claim rests on every member being byte-faithful, which is asserted not proven for the union merge step.
faithfulness_risks.fix: Add a fixture-build assertion that every extractor output span is a verbatim substring of the fetched HTML's text (byte-for-byte, modulo a declared whitespace-normalization that is identical to production's clean_fetch_body). Fail the candidate if any line in its output is not a verbatim source span. Make this a GATE-0 check for the union candidate specifically.

faithfulness_risks.risk: SemHash semantic merge can FALSE-MERGE two distinct independent sources that paraphrase the same claim — silently deleting a real corroborator (the §-1.3 consolidate-keep-all violation). The design correctly identifies this as the precision arm, but the PORTING NOTE (wire winner in keep-all basket mode) is described, not tested by the isolation metric — so a winner could be adopted and then wired in DROP mode by mistake, reintroducing the incumbent's keep_first drop the design itself flags as a DNA collision.
faithfulness_risks.fix: The metric already guards false-merge well; the gap is the wire-in. Require the eventual port to ship a behavioral replay assertion (§-1.4) that on a real corpus_snapshot the deduped output retains ALL source_urls per merged cluster as multi-citation (collapsed corroboration WEIGHT, zero dropped URLs), failing loud if any source_url disappears.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: drb_gold_sources.jsonl does not exist — per-finding title->canonical-URL/DOI resolution for ~50-60 named studies across idx 56/62/66/72. The design confirms info_recall items name studies by title with ZERO embedded URLs.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Resolve each quoted study title via Crossref + OpenAlex title lookup, verify the DOI resolves AND the returned title matches the rubric title (fail loud on ambiguity, never guess), exclude the DRB-II blocked source, sha-pin in the lineage manifest. CRITICAL ADD per the violation above: capture a per-finding equivalent-source SET, not one URL.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture (~60-100 URLs) with human-labeled main-content reference spans (WebMainBench/MainWebBench protocol) AND gold recovery verdicts — corpus snapshots store URLs+tier-reasons only, NOT reference bodies.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Stratified-sample real URLs from the banked classified_sources, hand-label main-content span + recovery class per URL, re-snapshot raw HTML per URL to freeze the wall/stub state, seed with the already-banked AEA-real + scirp-shell bodies as GATE-0 rows. Store under tests/fixtures/fetch_crawl_refbody/.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: The clinical extraction fixture (~50-100 pages with gold main-body Markdown AND gold table trees for TEDS) does not exist; only the general WebMainBench reuse is in hand. Also an open question whether WebMainBench ships an official eval script.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Hand-label real pages across POLARIS source types (journal HTML, FDA label, EMA SmPC, ClinicalTrials.gov, guideline, preprint), oversample table-bearing pages. Confirm/obtain the official ROUGE-N scorer; if absent, re-implement to the paper config and tolerance-check against published Table-2 anchors (this is the strongest layer — anchors are external ground truth).

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl (~300-500 sources, >=150 authoritative + >=150 on-topic-spam, ~15-25 topics) with dual-human labels and kappa. MISSING from the spec: within-source-TYPE pairs, and an explicit statement that 'authoritative' labels are independent of POLARIS tier vocabulary.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Draw from banked corpus_snapshot.json, dual-annotate authoritative vs on-topic-spam by reading body text (tier vocabulary withheld from annotators), report kappa. Add same-register pairs. Persist under tests/fixtures/.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: Labeled near-dup PAIR fixture (~300-500 bodies, hard-negative + true-positive strata) — snapshots carry bodies but NO per-pair labels. Ground-truth gap: labels are dual-annotated by Claude+Codex (AI), NOT human, in a clinical-safety context.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Assemble adversarial strata from the 6 banked snapshots; dual-annotate per-pair with cited-span justification. For clinical §-1.1, escalate at least the hard-negative (distinct-same-claim) stratum to a human adjudicator since AI-vs-AI agreement is not the §-1.1 human-read standard; record who adjudicates each disagreement.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: Labeled relevance+credibility candidate-pool fixture (~4 gold idxs x ~800-1000 candidates + injected spam/off-topic exemplars), with credibility labels annotated INDEPENDENTLY of POLARIS tier field.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Extract pre-rerank pool from banked snapshots, relevance-label via DRB-II info_recall, credibility-label via an independent authority rubric (judge + Codex/human cross-audit, NOT tier), inject+label on-topic-spam and off-topic exemplars, freeze versioned with per-candidate gain {0,1,2,3}.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: Axis A label sets exist (keyword-derived, in relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py) but need human-read confirmation per the pattern-presence violation. Axis B (reasoning-retrieval recall@k on non-lexical evidence) fixture does NOT exist and the layer-7 metric/gate0 detail is truncated in the provided design — partially verified only.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: For Axis B, derive hand-verified (rubric-claim -> non-lexically-overlapping supporting source) pairs from the DRB-II expert rubrics for the 5 questions. Read the full I-ret-002 design / docs/standard_process_pipeline_section_review.md to audit the actual layer-7 metric+gate0 (truncated here). For Axis A, gate the keyword labels behind a human body-read.

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: All four canaries (positive/negative/normalization/lineage) feed SYNTHETIC ranked lists to the scorer — they prove the scorer math and the idx binding, but NOT that each search candidate actually LOADED and returned real varying output. An Exa call returning [] (key issue / use_autoprompt misconfig), an unkeyed candidate, or a SearXNG container not up would each score a BELIEVABLE LOW recall, not fail loud — the drb_72 'garbage that scores like a real number' wiring failure for which the metric-only canary is blind.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Add a per-candidate LIVENESS canary (the missing half, present only in quality_weight's Part B): before scoring, issue each backend a known-easy query whose top result is pre-known (e.g. 'WHO antimicrobial resistance' -> who.int) and ASSERT the candidate returns a non-empty ranked list containing it; a candidate returning empty/constant is marked NON-FUNCTIONAL and excluded, never scored 0.0 as if it discovered nothing.

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: GATE-0 feeds three pre-banked bodies to the SCORER, bypassing all four fetch engines — it validates the completeness scorer (good~1.0/trunc~0.6/junk~0.0) but proves nothing about whether each ENGINE actually fetches. Specifically, per fact_zyte_key_location memory, Zyte SILENTLY NO-OPS without ZYTE_API_KEY — an unkeyed Zyte would return walls/errors on every URL and score a real-looking low recovery, indistinguishable from Zyte genuinely failing to unblock.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Add a per-engine liveness+key assertion: assert ZYTE_API_KEY (and PG_ZYTE*) is present in the bake-off env and that Zyte returns a RECOVERED body on a known-fetchable control URL before any stratum is scored; same for FIRECRAWL_API_KEY; assert crawl4ai/Playwright return non-empty HTML on the control URL. A keyless or load-failed engine is marked non-functional, not scored.

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: GATE-0 Part B is strong on scorer-math (hand-computed NDCG, ideal=1.0, drop/recall-guard checks) but has NO per-candidate model-wiring liveness check. A reranker that load-fails to a constant score (trust_remote_code error, wrong template, OOM fallback) would still produce a real-looking NDCG ~ the base ordering, not fail loud.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Add a per-candidate wiring canary mirroring quality_weight Part B: feed each loaded reranker one obviously-relevant and one obviously-irrelevant doc for a fixed query and ASSERT it ranks relevant strictly above irrelevant AND the two scores DIFFER (not constant). Exclude any candidate that fails as non-functional.

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: Layer-7 gate0 is truncated in the provided design (cannot be fully audited). For Axis A, the same per-candidate liveness gap applies: an embedder that load-fails to constant/zero vectors yields AUC~0.5, indistinguishable from a genuinely signal-free model. The late-interaction PyLate path (MaxSim over a multi-vector index) is a distinct, more fragile wiring than single-vector cosine and needs its own load-success assertion.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Read the full design to audit the actual gate0, then ensure a per-candidate liveness canary: assert each embedder/MaxSim scorer separates one hand-built on-topic vs off-topic pair with differing (non-constant) scores before trusting its AUC; assert the PyLate index built and returned per-token vectors (not empty) for the late-interaction candidates specifically.

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: Strongest gate0 in the set (it IS the model that has the per-candidate Part-B wiring canary the others lack — use it as the template). Residual gap: Part B uses one authoritative + one garbage doc; a model could pass that single pair yet be near-random on the real fixture, and the metric canary's all-tied=0.5 check does not catch a model that loaded but is mis-prompted.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Minor: broaden Part B to a handful (3-5) of obvious authoritative/garbage pairs per candidate and require correct ranking on all, to reduce the chance a lucky single pair passes a mis-wired model.

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: GATE-0 is bidirectional and strong (positive byte-identical, positive distinct, no-op recall floor, over-merge precision/false-merge floor) — it tests scorer math well. Gap: like the others it feeds the SCORER, not each dedup MECHANISM end-to-end; a candidate whose library import/threshold-sweep silently produced empty clusters would behave as the no-op control and score recall=0.0 (correctly low) but be misread as 'this method is bad' rather than 'this method did not run'.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Minor: add a per-candidate functional assertion that on the two byte-identical control bodies the LIVE candidate (not a stub) actually emits a cluster of size 2, distinguishing 'method ran and chose not to merge' from 'method failed to run'.

critics.bottom_line: This is a careful, mostly §-1.1-compliant design — content_extraction is genuinely strong (external published-number anchors as ground truth), and most metrics are real recall/AUC/NDCG against labeled gold rather than banned counts. It is NOT yet ready to write into a Codex-gated brief: there are two material defects that must be fixed first — (1) the reranker's 'calibrated off-topic-drop' sub-metric is a NEW hard FILTER the bake-off would optimize for, a direct §-1.3 weight-not-filter breach (verified: the incumbent _rerank_and_reserve only count-cuts, so this drop is genuinely new), and (2) search_discovery's single-canonical-gold_url scoring violates §-1.3 basket-faithfulness (a valid alternate source scores 0). The single biggest risk, though, is GROUND-TRUTH: every one of the seven layers is blocked on a net-new, unbuilt fixture, and three of them have ground-truth-quality concerns (single-URL gold not a source-set; quality_weight winnable by source-TYPE register unless paired within-type; dedup gold labeled by Claude+Codex AI rather than humans in a clinical-safety context). Compounding it: most gate0 canaries test scorer-MATH on synthetic inputs but lack the per-candidate LIVENESS canary (only quality_weight has it) — so an unkeyed Zyte, an empty Exa list, or a load-failed reranker/embedder would score a believable-low number instead of failing loud, which is precisely the drb_72 wiring class the whole process exists to stop. Fix the reranker drop, make the search gold a per-finding source-set, add per-candidate liveness canaries everywhere, escalate the dedup hard-negative labels to human adjudication, and confirm quality_weight/reranker credibility labels are independent of POLARIS's own tier metadata — then it is brief-ready. (Note: layer-7 embedder was truncated in the input; its metric/gate0 were only partially verifiable and should be re-audited against the full design file before briefing.)

faithfulness_risks.risk: Axis-A AUC uses keyword-defined POS/NEG label sets (the existing relevance_scorer_bakeoff LABEL_SETS: e.g. drb_72 POS=AI AND labor keywords, NEG=ptsd/colorectal/parkinson). The SCORE being ranked is the embedder's query<->body similarity (a real quality signal, not a banned count), so this is NOT a banned-proxy violation. But the GROUND-TRUTH LABELS are keyword-membership, so a source genuinely on-topic without the registry keyword is mislabeled NEG and an off-topic source containing the keyword is mislabeled POS — the AUC then measures 'agreement with a keyword heuristic', not true on/off-topic separation. Verified: LABEL_SETS in scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py drives label_rows() and only covers the banked slugs.
faithfulness_risks.fix: Treat keyword labels as a coarse pre-filter, then have a judge (or human) confirm the borderline POS/NEG rows the design already says to print and eye-read (§-1.1); report the count of keyword-only vs judge-confirmed labels per question so the AUC's label validity is auditable, not assumed.

faithfulness_risks.risk: GATE-0 check #3 (reproduce Dripper Table-2 anchors: Trafilatura ~0.6402, MinerU ~0.8779, etc.) is the strongest harness-integrity gate in the design IFF an OFFICIAL WebMainBench ROUGE-N(N=5, jieba) scorer exists. The design's own 'honest open item (a)' admits it may not ship one, in which case the fallback is to RE-DERIVE the scorer and tolerance-check against the same published numbers — which is self-fitting/circular (you tune the re-implementation until it reproduces the anchors, so the anchors can no longer independently validate it).
faithfulness_risks.fix: Before building, confirm opendatalab/WebMainBench ships an executable eval script. If yes, GATE-0 #3 is sound. If no, have an independent party (Codex) re-derive the scorer blind before comparing to anchors, or downgrade #3 to an advisory band and rely on the non-circular #1 (perfect-input->F1~1) and #2 (junk->F1~0) checks.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: drb_gold_sources.jsonl does not exist and is genuinely net-new. VERIFIED in primary source: DRB-II info_recall items carry ZERO embedded URLs (idx56=57 items/0 urls, idx62=23/0, idx66=48/0, idx72=47/0); they name studies by quoted title. The title->canonical-URL/DOI resolution must be built before any recall@k can be computed. The blocked-reference (content.blocked) DOES carry urls (verified, e.g. idx72 has pubmed+mdpi urls) so the make_blocked_filter exclusion path is real.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Build the fixture as the design states (Crossref+OpenAlex title lookup, verify DOI resolves and title matches, fail-loud on ambiguity, sha-pin). Single hard prerequisite for the layer.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: FALSE 'reuse' claim. The metric promises cross-host mirror matching ('a publisher mirror / ?utm / trailing-slash variant still matches') and claims to 'reuse harness _normalize_url + DOI-canonicalization'. VERIFIED: the existing _normalize_url (qgen_coverage_harness.py:95) only strips scheme/www/query/fragment/trailing-slash on the SAME host. It does NOT canonicalize a DOI across hosts (doi.org/10.x vs aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.x vs pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/NNN are different strings and will NOT match). Without cross-host DOI canon, a candidate that surfaces the gold paper on a different host scores 0 — the metric is not measurable as designed. The DOI-canon piece does not exist and must be BUILT.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Build a DOI/identifier-keyed matcher: resolve each ranked URL to a DOI/PMID (Crossref/Unpaywall/regex) and match on the canonical identifier, not the host string. Reclassify in the brief as a build item, not a reuse.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: idx66 gold-source denominator collapses. VERIFIED: only 3 of 48 idx66 info_recall items carry a quoted title; the other 45 are untitled prose findings. Under the design's own 'never guess, fail loud on ambiguity' rule, untitled findings cannot be resolved to a gold source and drop out, so idx66 recall is computed over ~3-6 gold sources vs idx56's ~14 — a thin, unrepresentative number (drb_72 in a new dress). Same title->source gap hits the reranker relevance labels (a candidate 'supplies info_recall point f' requires resolving f to a source).
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Resolve gold at the CLAIM level not the title level (judge maps each untitled finding to its supporting source) and report per-idx gold-N so the thin denominator is visible; or explicitly mark idx66's number low-confidence. Blocks the validity of the idx66 score specifically.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: Largest judge-labeling lift. The credibility half needs an INDEPENDENT authority annotation over the full pre-rerank pool. VERIFIED pool size: ~849 classified_sources / ~797 evidence_rows PER idx x 4 idx = ~3,200-4,000 candidates each needing relevance+credibility labels. Tier field exists but is the banned self-grading the design correctly excludes.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Budget the ~3.4k-candidate judge-annotation explicitly (parallel judge runs, Codex/human cross-audit on a sample per §-1.1.5), reuse the claim-level resolution from the search fix, report per-idx label coverage.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl: 300-500 sources HUMAN dual-annotated authoritative-vs-on-topic-spam with inter-annotator kappa, labels independent of tier_classifier/authority_model AND of the 5 candidates. Largest pure-human labeling lift (no existing label source; the snapshot tier field is the excluded self-grading). On-topic-spam class must also be ASSEMBLED — the natural pool under-represents pure on-topic SEO-spam (it dies at upstream filters).
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Plan human dual-annotation as a discrete work item with a kappa target and an independent labeler pair; pre-source on-topic-spam exemplars. No shortcut — a model-labeled fixture would be circular against the model candidates.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: HARD METRIC-VS-BUILD CONTRADICTION. The metric says precision/recall are computed 'EXHAUSTIVELY over every labeled pair... not a sample' over ~300-500 bodies. VERIFIED arithmetic: C(300,2)=44,850 and C(500,2)=124,750 pairs. The build estimate is '~0.5 day of dual-annotation'. 45k-125k dual-annotated+adjudicated pairs is impossible in 0.5 day — the 'exhaustive' claim and the 0.5-day estimate are mutually exclusive; as written the layer cannot be built.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Reconcile before briefing: either (a) shrink the exhaustively-labeled body set to N~50-80 (C(80,2)=3,160 pairs, dual-annotatable) and state the metric is exhaustive over that smaller frozen set, or (b) use a candidate-proposed-pair blocking protocol (label pairs any candidate merges + a sampled negative set) which VOIDS the 'exhaustive over all C(N,2)' wording and must be re-described honestly.

ground_truth_gaps.what_missing: Reference BODIES are net-new for both layers. VERIFIED: corpus_snapshot retrieval.classified_sources rows carry only {url,title,tier,tier_reasons,domain} — NO body. So fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture (60-100 URLs, human main-content span + gold recovery verdict) and the clinical extraction fixture (50-100 pages, gold main body + gold table trees for TEDS) are both from-scratch human-labeling builds. evidence_for_gen DOES carry direct_quote bodies (~1.3k chars, useful for seeding the GATE-0 canary rows but not a full main-content reference span). The design correctly flags both as needs_fixture.
ground_truth_gaps.how_to_get_it: Both fixtures need the WebMainBench/MainWebBench human main-content marking protocol; budget as discrete labeling work. fetch_crawl correctly notes live-page drift (re-snapshot raw HTML at build time, score wall/stub strata against the frozen snapshot).

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: SYSTEMIC GATE-0 GAP (the single biggest weakness): these three layers' canaries validate only the SCORER on SYNTHETIC inputs (synthetic ranked URL lists, banked pre-known bodies, hand-computed mini-fixtures). NONE asserts that a LIVE candidate adapter returns real, varying, non-stub output for a known-easy query — exactly the 'Title-only-stub harness bug' the mandate names. The design ITSELF documents the failure: Zyte 'silently no-ops without ZYTE_API_KEY' (memory-confirmed historical incident) and Exa 'must use use_autoprompt=False or it rewrites the query' (a holding-condition break a synthetic-scorer canary cannot see). A stubbed/keyless/misconfigured candidate returns empty or degraded, scores a real-looking LOW number, and is misread as 'this backend is bad at discovery' — a trustworthy-looking wrong number, the drb_72 class. By contrast quality_weight (Part B: each loaded model must rank authoritative>garbage AND scores must differ) and dedup (no-op stub->recall=0, over-merge stub->false-merge=1) DO test candidate wiring — proving the pattern is known and just omitted on three layers.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Port the per-candidate LIVENESS smoke from quality_weight Part B / dedup to search_discovery, fetch_crawl, reranker: before scoring, feed each live candidate ONE known-easy input (a query whose top result is obvious / a URL whose body is banked) and ASSERT it returns a real, non-stub, query-appropriate, VARYING result; fail loud and exclude as non-functional rather than scoring it. For search, also assert Exa did not rewrite the held-fixed query (compare the issued query) and Zyte/Firecrawl keys are present-and-firing (grep the response, per the Zyte memory).

gate0_weaknesses.weakness: GATE-0 check #3 (reproduce Dripper published per-extractor anchors) is potentially circular — see faithfulness_risks: if no official WebMainBench scorer exists, the re-derived scorer is tuned until it hits the same published numbers, so the anchors stop being an independent validator.
gate0_weaknesses.fix: Confirm the official scorer exists first; if not, make the re-derivation blind/independent before comparing to anchors, or downgrade #3 to advisory and rely on the non-circular #1 (perfect-input->1.0) and #2 (junk->0.0) checks.

critics.bottom_line: Not ready to write into a Codex-gated brief as-is. The design is genuinely §-1.1/§-1.3 disciplined (no banned-proxy violations — it pre-empts deduplication_ratio and frames every metric as recall/AUC/NDCG against labels, weight-not-filter intact), and its load-bearing primary-source claims I checked are TRUE (gold sources don't exist, corpus rows carry no bodies, evidence_for_gen has direct_quote bodies, the slug->idx offset and blocked-ref loader are real). It is needs_fixes_MATERIAL not minor because two findings each produce a confident-but-untrustworthy number — the drb_72 standard for 'material'. Biggest risk: three layers (search_discovery, fetch_crawl, reranker) have GATE-0 canaries that test only the scorer on synthetic inputs and never assert a live candidate returns real non-stub output — and the design itself documents the exact stub failure modes (Zyte silent no-op without key, Exa query-rewrite) those canaries are blind to; a stubbed candidate scores a real-looking LOW number misread as a genuine result. Close second: idx66's gold denominator collapses to ~3-6 sources (only 3 of 48 info_recall items carry a resolvable title) yielding a thin, unrepresentative recall, and the dedup metric's 'exhaustive over all C(N,2) pairs' is arithmetically impossible (45k-125k pairs) against its '0.5-day' build estimate. All four high-labeling/high-build fixtures (quality_weight human, reranker ~3.4k judge, both refbody fixtures) plus the false 'reuse _normalize_url+DOI-canon' claim must be fixed before briefing; none require a redesign — they are scoped additions (port the liveness canary, claim-level gold resolution, reconcile the dedup pair-count, budget the human/judge labeling).
